name: In Vivo High-Resolution Inner Ear Imaging Grant Application

sections.name: project_summary
sections.title: Project Summary
sections.instructions: Provide a concise overview of the proposed project, including the specific aims, the imaging technology to be developed or improved, and the expected impact on inner ear diagnosis and treatment.

sections.name: significance_innovation
sections.title: Significance and Innovation
sections.instructions: Discuss the significance of the proposed research in the context of current scientific challenges and opportunities. Evaluate the importance of the proposed research for advancing knowledge within the field, or more broadly. Evaluate how the proposed improved technology or techniques advance imaging of the human inner ear.  Assess how the new technologies or techniques will be state-of-the-art, and the advantages or disadvantages that this advance represents.

sections.name: approach
sections.title: Approach
sections.instructions: Describe in detail the experimental design and methods, discussing the imaging techniques and tools, including hardware and software. Explain plans for data analysis, image reconstruction and processing, and the rationale for the chosen approach. Evaluate how feasible the plans for reaching the desired increase in resolution are. Evaluate the challenges that may prevent the imaging techniques from being translated to use in humans and the proposed timeline as well. Evaluate the possible pitfalls with the timeline and proposed necessary steps for bringing the imaging techniques into use with humans, and to a clinical setting.

sections.name: investigators_environment
sections.title: Investigators and Environment
sections.instructions: Describe the qualifications of the research team, the available resources to conduct the proposed research, and assess how well the PD(s)/PI(s) are positioned to make significant leaps forward in imaging resolution. If a multidisciplinary team is proposed, evaluate how well team members complement each other's expertise and skill sets in the proposed work?

sections.name: human_subjects_vertebrate_animals
sections.title: Protections for Human Subjects and/or Vertebrate Animals
sections.instructions: If the proposed research involves human subjects, provide justification for their involvement.  If research involves human subjects or vertebrate animals, evaluate the justification for involvement of human subjects and the proposed protections from research risk relating to their participation. If the proposed research includes Vertebrate Animals, evaluate the involvement of live vertebrate animals

sections.name: data_management_sharing_plan
sections.title: Data Management and Sharing Plan
sections.instructions: Describe the plan for data management and sharing, including how the data will be stored, accessed, and shared with the research community, consistent with the NIH policy for data management and sharing.
